name: Lemon Tea Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the lemony glaze and set aside.
2. In a large bowl, cream together butter, sugar, and lemon extract until fluffy.
3. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
4. Sift in flour, baking powder, and salt alternately with milk into the egg mixture.
5. Stir in 1/3 of the flour mixture first, then add 1/3 of the milk.
6. Continue alternating until all ingredients are blended; do not overmix.
7. Fold in lemon peel and pecans.
8. Pour batter into prepared loaf pan.
9. Bake for 60 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean.
10. Remove bread from the oven and pour the lemony glaze slowly over the top.
11. Let cool on a rack for 15-20 minutes before turning out to serve.
Before serving, allow the bread to come to room temperature. Slice thinly and serve with butter, lemon curd, or clotted cream (optional).
Variation:
For finger-food sized slices at parties, bake in smaller loaf pans.
For Vegans:
Margarine can be used instead of butter. Dairy milk substitutes may also work, though the texture might differ slightly.
